title: Wash Rotom Battle Guide
summary: An [Electric]/[Water] special pivot with [levitate] — only one effective weakness ([Grass]), making it one of the most reliable pivots in the format.
weaknesses: 50 HP is low despite good defenses
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Wash Rotom has arguably the best defensive profile of any special pivot in the format. [Electric]/[Water] typing plus [levitate] leaves only a single effective weakness — [Grass]. This lets Wash Rotom switch into nearly anything and still deal meaningful damage back.

## Strengths
A single weakness means Wash Rotom is almost always a safe pivot. [hydro-pump] hits hard enough to threaten [Ground] and [Rock] attackers, [volt-switch] maintains pivot momentum, and [thunder-wave] cripples faster offensive threats. The defensive typing alone makes it a staple in support-oriented teams.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The only live weakness is [Grass], but [choice-specs] [leaf-storm] users can still score KOs. 50 HP caps the total bulk, so being outside of [sitrus-berry] range is dangerous. [mold-breaker] users bypass [levitate].

## Recommended Moveset
[hydro-pump] is the main [Water] STAB. [volt-switch] is the momentum move. [thunder-wave] is the utility slot — critical for speed control. [protect] is standard.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sitrus-berry] is the default for bulky sets. [choice-specs] maximizes damage when the team needs a special nuke. [levitate] is mandatory. Tera [Water] stacks STAB and removes the [Grass] weakness (becomes neutral).

## Teammates & Synergy
Pivot cores love Wash Rotom. Cover [Grass] with [Fire], [Flying], or [Bug] partners. [intimidate] support extends physical bulk.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on almost anything — [Ground], [Fire], [Steel], [Rock] all fail to threaten. Use [volt-switch] for momentum, [hydro-pump] when committing to damage, and [thunder-wave] to slow down dangerous threats. Avoid [Grass] attackers at all costs.
